entries.title: Machine Group Policy processing started
entries.summary: The Group Policy client began processing machine (computer) policy.
entries.details: Generated when the Group Policy client starts a machine policy processing cycle. Machine policy processing occurs at startup and at regular intervals (default: 90 minutes with 0-30 minute random offset). This event records the reason for processing (startup, periodic, or manual) and the machine account context.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: Check whether processing was triggered outside the normal cycle — 'reason for processing' field distinguishes scheduled from forced refreshes
Correlate timestamp with process creation events to see if gpupdate.exe was run by an unexpected account
A forced refresh immediately after GPO changes recorded in Active Directory replication logs is a strong T1484.001 indicator
commonFalsePositives: Normal periodic machine policy processing every 90 minutes
IT administrators running gpupdate /force during maintenance

entries.title: User Group Policy processing started
entries.summary: The Group Policy client began processing user policy for a logged-on user.
entries.details: Generated when user Group Policy processing begins for a logged-on account. User policy processing occurs at logon and periodically while the session is active. The event records the user account, whether it is background processing, and the reason for processing.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: IsBackgroundProcessing=false (foreground/logon processing) combined with an unexpected logon event is notable
PrincipalSamName for privileged accounts (Domain Admins) having user policy unexpectedly refreshed warrants investigation
Pair with EID 5313 to see which user GPOs were actually applied
commonFalsePositives: Normal user logon triggering user policy processing
IT administrators running gpupdate /force /target:user for troubleshooting

keyFields.name: PrincipalSamName
keyFields.description: The user account whose policy is being processed

keyFields.name: IsBackgroundProcessing
keyFields.description: Whether this is background (periodic) or foreground (logon) processing

entries.title: Machine Group Policy processing completed successfully
entries.summary: Machine Group Policy processing finished without errors.
entries.details: Generated when a machine Group Policy processing cycle completes successfully. Records the machine account, processing time, count of applied GPOs, and whether processing was foreground or background. A change in the NumberOfGroupPolicyObjects count compared to previous cycles may indicate a GPO was added, removed, or that the machine's OU membership changed.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: Track NumberOfGroupPolicyObjects over time — a new GPO appearing in the count warrants checking EID 5312 for the new GPO name
PolicyElapsedTimeInSeconds significantly higher than baseline may indicate a new, complex extension being processed (e.g., a new logon script GPO)
Correlate this event with EID 7016 absence — a clean 5016 after a previous 7016 failure may indicate a policy fix or a policy that was re-applied
commonFalsePositives: Normal successful machine policy refresh — fires every 90 minutes by default

keyFields.name: PrincipalSamName
keyFields.description: The machine account that completed policy processing

keyFields.name: PolicyElapsedTimeInSeconds
keyFields.description: Time spent processing policy; significant increases may indicate a new complex extension

keyFields.name: NumberOfGroupPolicyObjects
keyFields.description: Count of GPOs applied during this cycle

keyFields.name: IsBackgroundProcessing
keyFields.description: Whether processing was background (periodic) or foreground (startup)

entries.title: User Group Policy processing completed successfully
entries.summary: User Group Policy processing finished without errors.
entries.details: Generated when user Group Policy processing completes successfully. Records the user account, processing time, and count of applied GPOs. A change in GPO count compared to previous cycles may indicate a new or removed GPO in the user policy scope.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: Compare NumberOfGroupPolicyObjects at logon against historical baseline for the user
A new GPO applying logon scripts or software installation to a privileged user is high priority
Correlate with EID 5313 to identify specific GPO names in the applied set
commonFalsePositives: Normal user logon GPO processing — fires at each logon and periodically

keyFields.name: PrincipalSamName
keyFields.description: The user account that completed policy processing

keyFields.name: NumberOfGroupPolicyObjects
keyFields.description: Count of user GPOs applied during this cycle

entries.title: List of applicable machine Group Policy Objects
entries.summary: The complete list of Group Policy Objects applied during machine policy processing.
entries.details: Generated alongside EID 5016 and records the explicit list of GPOs applied during machine policy processing. Provides a complete snapshot of GPO names, GUIDs, precedence order, and enabled state for each processing cycle. This is the primary Group Policy event for tracking which GPOs are applied to a machine over time.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: Diff the GPO list between consecutive processing cycles — a new GPO GUID appearing is the primary T1484.001 indicator
Locate any new GUID in SYSVOL (\\domain\SYSVOL\domain\Policies\{GUID}) and review the policy scripts and settings
A GPO with a generic, misspelled, or unusual name warrants immediate review
commonFalsePositives: Legitimate new GPOs added by IT during authorized configuration changes
OU membership changes causing different GPOs to apply

entries.title: List of applicable user Group Policy Objects
entries.summary: The complete list of Group Policy Objects applied during user policy processing.
entries.details: Generated alongside EID 5017 and records the explicit list of GPOs applied during user policy processing. This is the user-policy equivalent of EID 5312, providing GPO names, GUIDs, and precedence order. The user context makes it possible to determine whether a GPO targets all users, a specific group, or a specific user account.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: A GPO appearing in the user policy list that contains a logon script setting is the most common T1484.001 persistence vector
Locate newly appearing GPO GUIDs in SYSVOL and review scripts under \\domain\SYSVOL\domain\Policies\{GUID}\User\Scripts\
GPOs with unexpected names, especially those that appear only for privileged user accounts, warrant immediate investigation
commonFalsePositives: New IT-managed user policy GPOs being rolled out
User OU membership changes causing a different set of GPOs to apply

entries.title: Machine Group Policy processing completed with errors
entries.summary: Machine Group Policy processing finished but one or more extensions reported errors.
entries.details: Generated when machine Group Policy processing completes with one or more client-side extension failures. Records the machine account, the extension that failed, and the error code. Common legitimate failure causes include network unavailability during startup processing, unreachable SYSVOL shares, or Group Policy preferences targeting offline resources.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: Identify the failing extension — Security Settings failures are higher priority than Preferences failures
A Security Settings extension failure may indicate the policy file in SYSVOL was tampered with or corrupted
Correlate with network connectivity events if the failure is SYSVOL-related — could indicate isolation or disruption
commonFalsePositives: Network unavailability during machine startup processing (SYSVOL not yet reachable)
Group Policy preferences targeting resources that are offline (drive maps, printer connections)

keyFields.name: PrincipalSamName
keyFields.description: The machine account whose policy processing failed

entries.title: User Group Policy processing completed with errors
entries.summary: User Group Policy processing finished but one or more extensions reported errors.
entries.details: Generated when user Group Policy processing completes with extension failures. Records the user account whose policy failed, the failing extension, and the error code. User policy failures are often benign (preferences targeting offline resources) but are significant when they affect security-relevant extensions such as Scripts or Folder Redirection.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification

investigationPivots: PrincipalSamName for a privileged account with extension failures warrants investigation
Scripts extension failures combined with a recently modified logon script GPO may indicate a malicious script that failed to run
Check the error code — STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED on a script or software installation path may indicate the payload file was removed by AV
commonFalsePositives: Drive map preferences targeting offline file servers
Software installation GPOs targeting packages that have already been installed or removed

keyFields.name: PrincipalSamName
keyFields.description: The user account whose policy processing failed
